description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain texture and a dark, weathered finish. The axe head is made of metal, displaying a broad, wedge-shaped blade with a single sharp edge and a flat poll on the opposite side. The blade is dark, possibly stained or painted, with visible red discoloration along the edge, suggesting either wear, paint, or residue. The handle is securely fitted into the eye of the blade, with the top of the handle protruding slightly above the head. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. This axe is typical of those found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or used for landscaping and emergency purposes.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a dark, broad metal blade, featuring red discoloration along the edge. The design is utilitarian and weathered, suitable for urban tool use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the curved wooden handle and the broad, dark metal blade with red discoloration.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the narrow profile of the blade and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the other face of the blade and handle curvature.
description_view_3: View from the top of the handle, showing the blade's thickness and the handle's cross-section.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural grain, oval cross-section, and no added grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a flat poll and broad cutting edge, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle inserted through the eye of the blade.
